objective: Stabilize codex-mem MCP server lifecycle so Codex desktop sessions stop accumulating stale mcp-server child processes and same-data-dir replacement works deterministically.

intent_contract.objective: Stabilize codex-mem MCP server lifecycle so Codex desktop sessions stop accumulating stale mcp-server child processes and same-data-dir replacement works deterministically.
success_criteria: The MCP server exits promptly when its stdio parent disconnects.
Starting a second mcp-server against the same data dir replaces the first one safely.
Runtime lock release is owner-safe and cannot delete a newer owner lock.
The fix is validated by focused shutdown/takeover tests, full npm test, and direct repro evidence.
non_goals: Do not change database repair behavior.
Do not modify the Codex desktop app.
Do not clean unrelated dirty work already present on the current branch.
authority_sensitive_decisions: Local workspace writes, local test execution, and localhost HTTP calls to the existing stack are authorized because the user explicitly requested implementation of this harness.

ambiguity_classification.statement: Deterministic extractive fixture generation must be heuristically derived from canonical article content because no existing full-corpus fixture generator exists.

constraints: Keep scope limited to src/mcp-server.ts, src/runtime-lock.ts, tests/mcp-server-shutdown.test.ts, tests/runtime-lock.test.ts, and governed artifacts.
Land on the current branch codex/scope-isolation-hardening and stage only the MCP stability scope.
Treat codex-mem retrieval evidence as unavailable in this session because the current desktop MCP transport is already closed.
scope_boundaries: Add the harness CLI, helper logic, governed artifacts, and focused unit tests.
Permit narrowly scoped shared-runner touchups only if needed to consume existing run_live_eval_pack(...) output safely.
Do not refactor unrelated orchestrator, review, serving, or observability code.
implementation_plan: Create the governed track and lock the harness artifact layout, local endpoint defaults, fixture identity rules, and additive write scope.
Implement corpus export plus existing-Q/A audit helpers and expose them through the new CLI.
Implement deterministic fixture generation, local preflight, eval persistence, miss extraction, and run comparison on top of existing runner contracts.
Add focused unit coverage for normalization, dedupe, fixture generation, comparison, miss extraction, and preflight failure conditions.
Run targeted validations and planning-gate closeout, recording adaptive-memory evidence and governed artifacts.

definition_of_done.criterion: The MCP server exits after parent stdin closes and a replacement server safely takes over the lock for the same data dir.
Focused shutdown/lock tests and the full codex-mem test suite remain green.
definition_of_done.verification: cd /Users/chadsimon/code/codex-mem && npm test -- --run tests/mcp-server-shutdown.test.ts tests/runtime-lock.test.ts && npm test

definition_of_done.criterion: Replacement logic only terminates a positively identified codex-mem mcp-server and fails closed otherwise.
Governed artifacts capture command evidence and finalize approval for the MCP stability fix.
The fix remains isolated to MCP lifecycle files and tests so a focused revert restores the prior runtime behavior.

objective_requirements.description: Export the active deduped snapshot into stable reusable corpus artifacts with exact counts and manifest identity.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The harness writes bundle.json, split JSONL corpus files, and summary.json from the active snapshot.
acceptance_checks: summary.json records exact counts and manifest hash
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: If corpus export is blocked, stop before fixture generation and preserve existing runtime and snapshot state.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier sees exact active snapshot identity and stable exported counts in the harness corpus artifacts.

objective_requirements.description: Audit existing Q/A rows from existing training and planning artifacts into a deterministic harness-local dedupe index.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The harness writes existing_qa_index.jsonl and summary.json using a stronger harness-local normalized question key.
acceptance_checks: summary.json records row counts and source breakdown
the harness-local key is distinct from existing repo-wide normalized_question_hash semantics
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: If audit scanning over-collects malformed rows, skip those rows and preserve explicit exclusion reasons.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier can trace normalized question keys and source counts from the audit output.

objective_requirements.description: Generate a deterministic reviewed eval fixture from canonical article content and run it through the local answer stack with strict preflight gating.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The harness writes fixture artifacts, passes local preflight, and persists judged local eval results from run_live_eval_pack(...).
acceptance_checks: harness_manifest.json includes fixture digest and snapshot id
preflight.json records required health and version checks
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: If preflight cannot verify the local stack or snapshot identity, stop before eval persistence and do not interpret partial results.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier sees preflight evidence, fixture identity, and judged runner output for the same snapshot and run id.

objective_requirements.description: Extract miss-only corrective training rows from judged results without mutating lifecycle state in the default harness flow.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The harness writes local_misses.jsonl and miss-training-export.jsonl for eligible failed rows only.
acceptance_checks: rows are limited to model_hallucination, prompt_contract_drift, and abstention_mistake
rows are deduped by the harness-local question key
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: If judged rows are incomplete or invalid, emit explicit skip reasons and do not call lifecycle materialization endpoints.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier can trace miss eligibility and dedupe decisions from persisted output and tests.

objective_requirements.description: Compare two harness runs deterministically only when their fixture identity matches.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The harness writes a comparison artifact covering pass/fail, answer-mode, and grounding-status deltas with fixture guardrails.
acceptance_checks: comparison hard-fails on mismatched fixture digest or case count
newly_failed and newly_fixed are reported by stable case_id
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: If fixture identities differ, stop comparison instead of emitting misleading deltas.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier can recompute the comparison basis from stored run manifests and row data.

objective_requirements.description: The implementation must close with auditable planning-gate artifacts, adaptive-memory evidence, and finalize ok=true.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The governed track contains validated plan and implementation artifacts plus finalize.json with ok=true.
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: If validate_impl or finalize_gate fail, stop with the governed gap isolated rather than asserting completion.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier sees planning-gate success with adaptive-memory evidence and no unresolved governed blockers.

assumptions_ledger.statement: The active snapshot available through export_snapshot_bundle(...) is the canonical source for this harness.
assumptions_ledger.disposition: Use the active snapshot by default and record its exact id, manifest hash, and counts in exported artifacts.

assumptions_ledger.statement: Deterministic extractive fixture generation can be derived from article/section/chunk text without a model call.
assumptions_ledger.disposition: Implement heuristic question templates and answer extraction from canonical section text only.

assumptions_ledger.statement: Executor version preflight can be satisfied through an explicit or derived executor admin URL rather than changing compose topology.
assumptions_ledger.disposition: Derive /internal/version from the configured or supplied serving upstream URL and fail closed if unreachable.

assumptions_ledger.statement: No project preference note currently overrides the requested harness behavior beyond existing governed constraints.
assumptions_ledger.disposition: Record successful codex-mem retrieval in governed evidence and proceed with the user-provided plan.

authority_map.scope: Add new harness code, tests, and governed artifacts in the current workspace and execute local validations against localhost services.
authority_map.resolution: Allowed because the user explicitly requested implementation of the approved harness plan.

verification: export-corpus command writes bundle and split JSONL outputs
run-local-eval persists preflight, local_answers, judge-row-results, and judge-summary outputs

integration_map.touchpoint: scripts/build_reviewed_eval_misses.py semantics + harness comparison logic -> corrective exports and comparison artifacts

evidence_plan.name: adaptive_memory_retrieval
evidence_plan.verification: Capture successful codex-mem retrieval and resolved preferences state in implementation evidence.

evidence_plan.name: harness_inputs_and_fixture
evidence_plan.verification: Persist exported corpus, existing-Q/A index, fixture outputs, and dedupe report under the governed track root.

evidence_plan.name: harness_run_artifacts
evidence_plan.verification: Persist preflight, answer rows, judged rows, summaries, misses, and comparison outputs for each run id.

dependencies: Existing snapshot export helper at services/common/snapshot_transfer.py
Existing local eval runner at scripts/official_docs_eval.py
Local stack endpoints reachable at localhost for orchestrator-api, review-ops, mcp-server, slm-runtime, slm-serving, and executor admin

smoke_gates.criteria: The MCP lifecycle sources compile into dist successfully.
smoke_gates.expected_output: The TypeScript build completes and dist/cli.js reflects the updated MCP lifecycle code.
smoke_gates.failure_interpretation: The MCP lifecycle changes are not compile-stable.
smoke_gates.proceed_decision: Proceed to focused shutdown/takeover tests only when build succeeds.
smoke_gates.rollback_decision: Fix source-level errors before expanding validation scope.

smoke_gates.criteria: Focused shutdown and lock regression tests pass.
smoke_gates.expected_output: The focused MCP shutdown, takeover, and owner-safe release tests pass.
smoke_gates.failure_interpretation: The targeted lifecycle behavior is still unstable.
smoke_gates.proceed_decision: Proceed to direct repro and full suite only when focused tests are green.
smoke_gates.rollback_decision: Tighten shutdown or lock semantics before broader validation.

smoke_gates.criteria: Direct repros confirm stdin-close exit and singleton takeover outside the Vitest harness.
commands: cd /Users/chadsimon/code/codex-mem && node direct singleton takeover repro
smoke_gates.expected_output: Standalone repros show prompt stdin-close exit and replacement lock ownership by the second process.
smoke_gates.failure_interpretation: The behavior only appears correct under tests and is not trustworthy in real launches.
smoke_gates.proceed_decision: Proceed to full suite and governed closeout only when direct repros pass.
smoke_gates.rollback_decision: Fix real process-lifecycle behavior before claiming stability.

smoke_gates.criteria: Full test suite and planning-gate finalize complete successfully.
smoke_gates.expected_output: Full npm test passes and finalize.json reports ok=true.
smoke_gates.failure_interpretation: The lifecycle fix or governed evidence is incomplete.
smoke_gates.proceed_decision: Treat the fix as approved only after full tests and finalize ok=true.
smoke_gates.rollback_decision: Close evidence or regression gaps before commit/push.

logging_plan: Keep all generated harness inputs, run outputs, and governed artifacts under planning_artifacts/task-20260312-codex-mem-mcp-stability/.
Persist fixture digest, snapshot id, run id, and preflight identity in machine-readable JSON rather than relying on chat summaries.
Capture validation commands through planning-gate run_cmd_capture.py so proof artifacts remain replayable.
rollback_plan: The change is additive; rollback is to stop invoking scripts/local_qa_regression_harness.py and remove new harness artifacts if needed.
Do not alter existing service contracts or lifecycle defaults while implementing the harness.
If shared runner changes become necessary, keep them minimal and revert only those additive touchpoints if regressions appear.
risks: Deterministic fixture heuristics may produce low-value prompts if section text selection is too loose or too repetitive.
Local preflight can fail if executor admin identity is not reachable from the host, even when the rest of the stack is healthy.
The workspace is already dirty in many shared files, so the harness implementation must avoid accidentally entangling unrelated changes.
non_goals: Do not change database repair behavior.
Do not modify the Codex desktop app.
Do not clean unrelated dirty work already present on the current branch.

quality_bar.maintainability: Keep the change isolated to MCP lifecycle entrypoints and runtime locking with explicit regression coverage.
quality_bar.evidence: Capture focused tests, full tests, and direct repros as planning-gate command artifacts.
quality_bar.policy_compliance: Planning-gate finalize ok=true is required before commit/push is treated as approved.

decomposition_policy.strategy: Build the harness in sequence: stable inputs first, deterministic fixture and eval execution second, misses/comparison third, then validation and closeout.

migration_fallback_policy.manifest_rollback_path: Keep the harness additive and stop on governed or validation blockers rather than widening scope.

gaps_detected: No reusable local full-corpus harness exists yet.
Deterministic full-corpus fixture generation must be implemented from canonical article content.
Local executor admin identity may require a derived or explicit URL for preflight.
gaps_auto_fixed: Locked the harness to additive CLI/helper code and governed artifacts under the task track.
Locked fixture identity matching as a hard comparison precondition.
Locked lifecycle mutation out of the default run path.

verifier_notes: The remaining work is additive code, focused tests, and governed closeout.

requirement_risk_rank.failure_impact: All later fixture generation would use the wrong corpus or missing identity data.
The generated fixture could duplicate existing training or eval content and pollute corrective exports.
The harness would not prove local answer behavior against a deterministic fixture.
Corrective training outputs would include wrong or duplicate rows, weakening downstream use.
Run deltas could be misleading or non-deterministic.
The work would lack auditable approval even if the code passes tests.

failure_mode_matrix.scenario: Fixture generation emits unstable or duplicate case ids across identical corpus inputs.
failure_mode_matrix.handling: Derive case ids from stable snapshot/version/section/question digests and cover them with unit tests.
failure_mode_matrix.verification: Focused harness tests prove deterministic ids and dedupe behavior.

failure_mode_matrix.scenario: Local preflight passes shallow health checks but misses serving/executor identity drift.
failure_mode_matrix.handling: Require authenticated serving and executor version checks and fail closed on snapshot mismatch.
failure_mode_matrix.verification: Preflight tests assert version and snapshot mismatch failures.

failure_mode_matrix.scenario: Miss extraction exports unsupported failure classes or mutates lifecycle state implicitly.
failure_mode_matrix.handling: Limit default export to eligible failure classes and keep lifecycle mutation out of the default code path.
failure_mode_matrix.verification: Miss extraction tests assert eligible-only filtering and absence of lifecycle side effects.

edge_case_matrix.scenario: Some planning artifact JSON rows contain instruction/response-like keys but are malformed or incomplete.
edge_case_matrix.handling: Skip malformed rows, count exclusions, and persist explicit reasons in the audit summary.
edge_case_matrix.verification: Audit tests cover malformed and partial row handling.

edge_case_matrix.scenario: The active snapshot contains sections with very short or low-information text.
edge_case_matrix.handling: Apply minimum-content heuristics and adaptive per-article density so trivial sections do not dominate the fixture.
edge_case_matrix.verification: Fixture generation tests cover short, medium, and long article densities.

edge_case_matrix.scenario: Two runs are compared with different fixture digests or case counts.
edge_case_matrix.handling: Abort comparison instead of emitting partial or misleading deltas.
edge_case_matrix.verification: Comparison tests assert hard failure on fixture mismatch.

packets.fallback_or_rollback: Stop before fixture generation if the additive input stages are not stable.

definition_of_done.behavior_outcome: The harness exposes stable corpus export and audit subcommands that emit reusable inputs under the governed track.
definition_of_done.rollback_or_fallback: Keep the change additive and stop before fixture generation if export/audit surfaces are incomplete.
definition_of_done.verifier_acceptance_condition: Verifier can trace corpus identity and audit output from the new CLI.
